title: Slice-tower convergence justified with the wrong connectivity invariant

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
In the paragraph 'That the tower converges (for $E$ connective ...)', convergence is justified by claiming 'the Postnikov connectivity of $G_+\otimes_H N^TS^1$ is $|T/H|\to\infty$ with $|T|$'.

Two problems:
(1) $|T/H|$ is the GEOMETRIC connectivity at the top, NOT the ordinary (Postnikov) connectivity. The underlying spectrum of $N^TS^1\cong S^{\mathbb R\cdot T}$ is $S^{|T|}$, so the relevant ordinary connectivity grows like $|T|$ (up to a factor from induction), not $|T/H|$.
(2) Convergence $E\xrightarrow{\sim}\varprojlim P^n_{\mathcal O}E$ for connective $E$ requires showing $\mathrm{conn}(P_{\mathcal O,n}E)\to\infty$ in the ordinary t-structure; this is asserted with an incorrect supporting invariant and without any estimate.

Suggested fix: prove $\tau^{\mathcal O}_{\ge n}\subseteq\mathrm{Sp}^G_{\ge\lceil n/|G|\rceil}$ (the underlying sphere of $G_+\otimes_H N^TS^1$ is built from $S^{|T|}$ with $|T|\ge n$, and induction lowers connectivity boundedly), then run the standard slice-tower convergence argument (HHR §4 / Hill primer).
